instrument_name: Playspace Play Value and Usability Audit Tool
preamble:
## How the tool is structured
The Playspace Play Value and Usability Audit tool is structured in 22 domains. Each domain has between 2 and 12 items. All items represent specific features or characteristics of the environment.
Each item is assessed through up to four scales: Provision, Diversity, Challenge Opportunities, and Sociability Support. Not all items include every scale.

### 1. Provision
Provision is always answered first. When the provision answer is a zero-like response such as No or Not applicable, the remaining scales stay hidden because they do not apply.

**Guiding question:** To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

### 2. Diversity
Diversity evaluates whether the provided items offer a variety of types, forms, or opportunities. In other words, whether they are not all the same.

**Guiding question:** To what extent is diversity in this feature/environmental characteristic considered?

Example: For 5 swings: Are they all different types (e.g. large swing, baby swing, basket swing, rope swing), or are they all identical?

**It is your judgment to indicate if there is No diversity, Some diversity, or A lot of diversity considered. You can only choose one.**

### 3. Challenge opportunities
Challenge opportunities assess whether the feature provides opportunities with different levels of difficulty.

**Guiding question:** To what extent does this feature/environmental characteristic offer different levels of challenge?

Example: The swings provided should be examined and judged to determine whether they provide increasing challenge opportunities.

**It is your judgment to indicate if the provided swings offer different levels of difficulty. You can only choose one.**

### 4. Sociability support
Sociability support assesses whether more than one child/person can use this feature/environmental characteristic together. It considers whether the feature can be used by more than one person at once, individually, in small groups, or in larger groups.

**Guiding question:** Can more than one child (or person) use this feature together?

Example: Can some of the swings be used alone? Can some be used in pairs? Can some be used in groups of children?

**It is your judgment to indicate if they can be used alone, in pairs, or in groups.**

## Open reflection
The open question gives you the possibility to write down reflections. A guiding question will ask you to describe one or two aspects you recommend changing in the playspace to increase its play value and usability.

## Two parts of the audit
The Playspace Play Value and Usability Audit tool uses two methods for assessing a playspace:

- **(A) Onsite Audit of the Physical Environment**
This part of the audit assesses aspects of the playspace that can only be examined onsite in the physical environment.
It requires being physically present at the playspace.

- **(B) Survey of Ecological Aspects of the Playspace**
This part of the audit assesses information related to the history, management practices, and how weather and seasons influence playspace use.
It does not require being onsite.
It must be answered by someone familiar with the playspace's background and context.

## Before you continue
Before moving to the next section, you must decide which part(s) of the audit you will complete: A, B, or A & B.

Your choice depends on how familiar you are with the playspace and what role you have.

execution_modes.label: I am both a playspace administrator/manager and an onsite auditor
execution_modes.description: This choice displays both survey and audit questions.

execution_modes.label: I am an administrator or manager of the playspace
execution_modes.description: This choice displays only survey questions.

execution_modes.label: I am an onsite auditor for this playspace
execution_modes.description: This choice displays only audit questions.

pre_audit_questions.label: Auditor ID
pre_audit_questions.description: Automatically populated from the auditor profile.

pre_audit_questions.label: Finish time of the audit
pre_audit_questions.description: Automatically generated when the final submit action is completed.

pre_audit_questions.label: Total time in minutes
pre_audit_questions.description: Automatically calculated from the start and finish timestamps.

options.label: Small
options.description: 1 to 2 activity zones only; e.g. found in a small neighbourhood park or housing complex

options.label: Medium
options.description: 2 to 5 activity zones; e.g. found in a moderate sized park or small school yard

options.label: Large
options.description: 6 to 10 activity zones; e.g. found in a large city park or large school yard

options.label: Very large
options.description: More than 10 activity zones; e.g. a large destination playspace

pre_audit_questions.label: How busy is the playspace currently?

scale_guidance.title: Provision
scale_guidance.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?
scale_guidance.description: Provision refers to the presence or quantity of an environmental feature or characteristic.

scale_guidance.title: Diversity
scale_guidance.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?
scale_guidance.description: Diversity evaluates whether the provided feature offers variety in type, form, or opportunity rather than all options being the same.

scale_guidance.title: Challenge Opportunities
scale_guidance.prompt: To what extent does this feature/environmental characteristic provide different levels of challenge?
scale_guidance.description: Challenge Opportunities assess whether the feature offers different levels of difficulty.

scale_guidance.title: Sociability Support
scale_guidance.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?
scale_guidance.description: Sociability Support assesses whether the feature can be used by more than one child or person together.

sections.title: Playspace Character & Community
sections.description: **Playspace Character & Community:** considers aspects of a playspace that offers opportunities for users to be involved with or identify with the playspace. These aspects relate to involvement in design and maintenance, local history/heritage/art/stories, unique characteristics, use for community events
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the character and community involvement of this playspace:

questions.prompt: was **developed or designed with the involvement of children and/or youth**

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: was **developed or designed with the involvement of other stakeholders** (e.g. parents; community organizations, staff)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **is staffed by adults who are actively facilitating children's play** (e.g. providing new opportunities in the environment for new /changing forms of play)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **involves users ** of the playground (children and/or adults) **in maintenance activities** (e.g., in landscaping, painting; assigns adults living nearby to keep an eye on the playspace)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **hosts community events**

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **reflects cultural and historical aspects of the community** (e.g., local art/stories/heritage is represented; locally sourced materials are used)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **has unique or novel aspects/opportunities** that distinguish it from other playspaces (e.g., highly naturalized playground; spaces or features that can be shaped and manipulated by children; sculptural play features)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

sections.title: Playspace Location & Connectivity
sections.description: **Playspace Location & Connectivity:** considers how the playspace is integrated into the local community, how users access the playspace, and proximity to other play/leisure opportunities.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the location and connectivity of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has **walking or biking paths leading to the playsapce entrance(s)**

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **parking for cars and bicycles close to the playspace**

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **accessible through public transport** (e.g., there is a public transit stop at or near at least one entrance)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **located near to a residential area** (within 10 min walk)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **located near to schools and/or childcare facilities** (within 10 min walk)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **located near to or co-located with other public opportunities** that might be interesting/important to children and families (e.g., park space; community centre; library; amenities; community gathering places; museums; community garden; nature areas; coffee shop)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **embedded in or connected to a larger naturalized space** (e.g. forest; woodland; large garden; conservation area)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **located directly adjacent to roads with heavy or fast traffic** without fencing and clear/safe crossing options

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Playspace Rules & Restrictions
sections.description: **Playspace Rules & Restrictions:** evaluates playspace rules or restrictions that can hinder outdoor play.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the rules and restrictions of this playsapace:

questions.prompt: **restricts certain age groups** from the playspace (e.g., older children, teens)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **restricts certain kinds of play or behaviour** (e.g., being loud, ball play; using wheeled play equipment or sleds)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **restricts children’s access by requiring adult supervision** (i.e., caregiver supervision is not mandatory, except perhaps for very young children; children can independently use the playspace)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Playspace Information & Wayfinding
sections.description: **Playsapce Information and Wayfinding:** considers information provided about the playspace and its play opportunities, and support for orientation and wayfinding.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the information and wayfinding of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has **online information** related to playspace use and features (e.g., opening hours, accessibility, transit options, parking)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has a **layout that is easy to navigate** (e.g. clear logical primary route from entry to exit; clearly defined play zones)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **accessible maps and/or signage at entrances** to the playspace that provide navigation and usage guidance (e.g. positioned at an accessible height for wheelchair users; uses Braille text, pictographs and/or high colour contrast; info available via QR code ) a

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **child-friendly, easy to read/understand maps and/or signage at entrances** (e.g. positioned at a height suitable for young children; uses simple language, symbols, and/or images)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **accessible maps and/or signage distributed around the playspace** that provide navigation and usage guidance (e.g. positioned at an accessible height for wheelchair users; uses Braille text, pictographs and/or high colour contrast; info available via QR code ) a

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **child-friendly, easy to read/understand maps and/or signage distributed around the playspace** (e.g. positioned at a height suitable for young children; uses simple language, symbols, and/or images)a

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Management & Maintenance
sections.description: **Management and Maintenance:** evaluates management and maintenance practices, as well as safety and cleanliness of the playspace througout the year .
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the management and maintenance of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has an **up-to-date safety certificate** for play equipment and/or surfaces

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **generally in good repair**, with few to no visible hazards such as broken or damaged equipment/surfacing, remnants from removed equipment, rotten or rough wood, peeling paint

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **mostly clean** and well-kept (e.g. little/no graffiti, cigarette butts, litter or other dangerous debris such as such as broken glass, syringes, animal feces, overgrown plants)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **sufficient litter bins** in relation to the size of the playspace and in relevant areas (e.g., at/near entry points; by table/seating areas)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **pervasive unpleasant smells**, including in enclosed spaces (e.g. from animal feces, smoke, and/or garbage)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **a storage area** for loose parts and/or ride-on equipment

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is **landscaped and maintained in a way that provides novel opportunities for play** (e.g., tunnels are cut into vegetation; low branches and boulders are left to climb on; has no-mow areas; fallen leaves/flowers/small branches are intentionally left for play; vegetation is not overly pruned)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

sections.title: Climate Protection & Adaptability
sections.description: **Climate Protection & Adaptability:** considers whether the playspace provides protection from weather/climate, and appropriate features for adapting to local conditions.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the climate protection and adaptability of this playsapace:

questions.prompt: provides **shade or shelter to most seating areas** at some point during the day Note to JL/TM: might be difficult to audit and might shift after piloting

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: provides **shade or shelter from sun/inclement weather in most primary play areas** at some point during the day (e.g. through trees/vegetation; buildings; shade structures or canopies; positioning of equipment). Note to JL/TM: might be difficult to audit and might shift after piloting

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: is designed and/or maintained to provide protection from inclement weather/sun/wind through **placement of features and/or landscaping** (e.g. wind screen provided by evergreen planting)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **appropriate lighting for the geographic location and local conditions** to ensure feelings of safety and to extend play times (e.g., lighting suitable for winter months ,evenings or changing weather conditions, and/or lit paths into the playspace)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **appropriate drainage** (through all seasons) that prevents flooding or large amounts of standing/stagnant water

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Boundaries & Entrances
sections.description: **Boundaries & Entrances:** considers entry to and separation of playspaces. Boundaries are defined as clear borders that illustrate the edges of an area or define distinct activity zones - these can include fences, walls or hedgerows. Entrances are defined as the locations where people enter/exit playspaces or activity zones - these can be formal (e.g., gates) or informal (e.g., openings in boundaries or changes in ground surface) Not every playspace needs fencing depending on where the playspace is located and what is close to the playspace (e.g., hazards in proximity). Fencing and lockable gates may contribute to the ease of caregivers by knowing their children are in a safe space to play (e.g., for families with younger children or children with disabilities). Not sure we need this explanation here - better in manual/guidelines - not needed to guide audit answers
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the bounderies and entrances of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has fences/boundaries **to separate play areas from significant hazards** such as road traffic or deep water

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has fences/boundaries **that are visually permeable**, allowing for easy observation/supervision into adjacent spaces

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: ... any **gates can be easily opened by adults** (including those with limited grip strength or from a wheelchair/mobility device)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: any **areas enclosed by full heigh** t boundaries/fencing have more than one entrance/exit

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Pathways
sections.description: **Pathways:** considers the availability & accessibility of pathways both for movement through the playspace and access to activity zones, but also as a play feature themselves. Primary paths are the main pathways that loop through the whole playspace and provide access to different features and zones - these tend to be wider and harder surfaced. Secondary paths are small and sometimes more informal trails that may branch off of main pathways or provide a short pathway within an activity zone - these tend to be narrower and may not be hardsurfaced (e.g., a narrow path lined with mulch, pebbles, or stepping stones. Both types of paths might also incorporate play opportunities such as curves, dips, bumps, tunnels, or support ability to run/roll/ride/walk over.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the pathways of this playspaces:

questions.prompt: has primary paths within the playspace that lead **directly into each play area / feature that are solid and level surfaced**, affording use by all users (including mobility devices)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

questions.prompt: has **paths within the playspace that provide novel/playful ride/run/walk experiences** (e.g. straight and winding paths or loops; paths with waves or bumps; paths that go through tunnels or gardens/wooded areas)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

questions.prompt: has **secondary paths that provide novel play routes** (e.g. lined up stumps; stepping stones through a garden or creek; play route through a willow tunnel or a play structure; a path mown in tall grass; a tunnel under a hill or berm; sensory-surface path)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

sections.title: Open Space
sections.description: **Open Space:** defined as an integrated area within or surrounding the playsapace that provides a fairly unobstructed, mostly flat surface. These tend to be multipurpose areas that can be used flexibly, affording a diversity of opportunities such as running, walking, sport or game play, loose parts play (e.g., with balls, hula hoops or natural elements like pinecones or tree cookies), sitting or relaxing, having picnics, playing in groups, etc.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the open spaces of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has **open, mostly flat spaces** (with little to no incline) that afford diverse play such as ball or sports play, group games or gathering

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

options.label: Yes - for a small group of users (2-10)
Yes - for a large group of users (11 or more)

questions.prompt: a portion of flat, open spaces have **accessible surfaces**, allowing wheeled devices such as wheelchairs or scooters to be used

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

options.label: Yes - for a small group of users (2-10)
Yes - for a large group of users (11 or more)

sections.title: Enclosed & Bounded Spaces
sections.description: **Enclosed & Bounded Spaces:** defined as an area with a clear boundary and which is fully or partly enclosed. These can be defined by natural and/or manufactured components. Examples include nooks, crannies, low raised boundaries framing a smaller space, play huts, dens, treehouses or other elevated spaces, etc.). Enclosed & Bounded Spaces afford children to be by themselves or play with a small group of children, to experience hiding, retreat or being away from adults and/or adult eyes. They often afford creative and imaginative play.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the enclosed and bounded spaces of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has enclosed or bounded spaces that afford **a sense of enclosure ** (e.g., house-like, room-like) or **that are fully enclosed** (e.g., playhouses; huts or dens; tree houses; enclosed small spaces formed by or under trees/shrubs, enclosed and bounded sapces which are elevated and on ground level)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

questions.prompt: has enclosed or bounded spaces that also **afford the opportunity to look out from and observe** without being overly visible from the outside (e.g. playhouse with small windows; boat feature with portholes; grass hut with small door opening)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity

questions.prompt: ... has play areas with **low raised boundaries / edges that define smaller spaces** (e.g., small niches; nooks; corners; sub-divided sand pits)

sections.title: Sport & Game Spaces
sections.description: **Sport & Game Spaces:** defined as areas that afford children engagement in rule-based group play such as soccer, basketball, (table) tennis, hopscotch or four-square. They typically have equipment and/or surfaces that support sport and games.These areas may be separated from other areas of the playspace, but in within or close to the playspace they should be included in this assessment.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the sport and game spaces of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has **spaces that can support sports or games** (e.g. soccer fields, basketball or tennis courts; table tennis; pavement games)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity

options.label: Yes - for a small group of users (2-10)
Yes - for a large group of users (11 or more)

questions.prompt: has sport/game spaces with **surfaces that are suitable for intended activities** (e.g. flat, open grass or pavement areas for soccer or basketball)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has sport/game spaces that have **suitable and well-maintained equipment or markings** for the activity (e.g. goal posts; basketball hoops & nets; surface markings for four square or hopscotch)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Manufactured Play Features
sections.description: **Manufactured Play Features:** refers to fixed, relatively large manufactured/built play structures (sometimes called play equipment or play components). They tend to afford diverse physical activities such as climbing, sliding and swinging. Manufactured play features may be built from diverse materials including plastic, metal, ropes/netting but also from wood.

sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the manifactured play features of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has manufactured play features which afford **swinging** (e.g. swings; hanging ropes; glider; monkey bars)

sections.title: Natural Play Features
sections.description: **Natural Play Features:** refers to fixed (planted or non-moving) natural elements located in or surrounding the playspace which are still in their natural form. Can include vegetation, shrubs/bushes, high grass, areas with trees, boulders/rocks, and or 'fallen' trees/logs. Natural features afford a variety of play opportunities, including physical active play (e.g.,, climbing), exploring, sensory play, manipulating, and creating their own play (e.g.,, socio-dramatic play or imagination), resting, being alone, or gathering with other children.
sections.instruction: Important: Your audit focuses on the natural play features provided and meant for play, NOT the features that are not accessible for play (e.g. trees that provide shade but are not accessible for climbing, sitting under, etc.). Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the natural play featuers of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has **large-scale vegetation** appropriate and accessible for play (e.g. climbing trees; large shrubs / hedges)

sections.title: Loose Manufactured Parts & Equipment
sections.description: **Loose Manufactured Parts & Equipment:** refers to smaller, human-made play objects such balls/frisbees or other toys, sand/mud/water play toys, bikes/scooters, go-carts. These may be always available in the playspace or stored onsite in bins/sheds.

sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the loose manifactured parts and equipment of this playspace:

questions.prompt: ... has **small manufactured loose parts ** available that are **light and easy to move/handle**, including those that can be used to manipulate malleable materials and other loose parts (e.g. cups; buckets; shovels; small tools; funnels; balls; light ropes; fabrics; balls; frisbees' hula hoops; rackets; toy cars/boats; puppets)

sections.title: Loose Natural Parts & Malleable Materials
sections.description: **Loose Natural Parts & Malleable Materials: refers to pliable or workable** materials like earth, mud, sand, and water or loose parts from natural features such as leaves, flowers, sticks, stones, cones, and others. Malleable materials afford sensory opportunities and afford mixing, digging, transporting, filling and emptying, building, etc. Natural loose parts afford collecting, arranging, creating, building, digging, mixing, explore, and can be used as props in socio-dramatic and creative play.

sections.title: Seating
sections.description: **Seating;** considers both formal and informal supports for sitting/resting. Seating might be used by children for resting, sitting, observing others, linger or socialize with peers but also afford evolving of creative and imaginative play. Adults may use seating for supervising children, resting, or participating in play. Formal seating includes benches, chairs, amphitheaters or picnic tables. Elements that provide informal seating opportunities include platforms, tree logs or stumps, steps, low raised boundaries, boulders/rocks, and hills).
sections.instruction: Answers about provision and diversity of seating should be considered against what is appropriate or expected for the size and/or type of playspace. Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve seating of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has opportunities to sit through **formal seating** (e.g. chairs; benches; stools; picnic tables)

questions.prompt: has opportunities to sit through **informal seating** (e.g. platforms/ledges; tree stumps; tree logs; boulders, low raised walls, places to sit or lie down that are integrated in larger play structures such as hammocks, comfortable nets or tower platforms)

sections.title: Topography & Surfaces
sections.description: **Topography & Surfaces: considers playspace ground surfaces and changes in the terrain that can support play. Topography elements can include** hills, slopes, depressions, and steps in the terrain that afford activities such as running up/down, rolling down, rolling things down, driving, climbing, balancing, sledding, sliding, enjoyment of an outlook or experience heights, jumping/diving/rolling in/over puddles, etc.). Ground surfaces can be made from different materials (such as grass/turf, rubber, mulch, sand) that afford diverse activities and/or sensory experiences.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve this playspace's topography and surfaces:

questions.prompt: has **topography ** that provides **differences in slope, incline, and/or height** (e.g. hills; berms; dips; valleys; ridges)

sections.title: Novelty
sections.description: **Novelty:** considers whether a playspace or its features provides new play opportunities or experiences by taking advantage of changes in weather, season or light or through use of interactive or sensor-triggered elements. Novel opportunities may also be provided through changes in height/prospect, elements that include a sense of mystery or invite exploration, or features/vegetation that attract (appropriate) wildlife. Novel features provide continually changing or evolving play opportunities or experiences.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the novelty of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has **features (by design) ** that provide **evolving play opportunities due to weather-responsive elements** (e.g., chimes or drums activated by wind or rain; dips that create temporary puddles after rainfall; dry creek beds that change during/after precipitation; hills and slopes suitable for sliding/sledding after snowfall)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: ... has features that provide **evolving play opportunities due to local seasonal changes** (e.g., leafy trees that change colors; trees that lose their leaves/seeds (e.g. acorns); fruit-bearing trees such as chestnuts/apples; flowering plants; ponds that freeze in winter; gardens with seasonal fruits and herbs)

sections.title: Sensory Qualities & Regulation Note to JL/TM: maybe it is "sensory qualitites and opportunitites for self regulation" OR only Sensory Qualitites. I had in an earlier Version the term "Sensory place realted qualitites"
sections.description: **Sensory Qualities & Regulation:** evaluates how well a space supports diverse sensory experiences that can enhance engagement, comfort, and well‑being. Considers the presence of visual, auditory, olfactory, and tactile elements that create interest and stimulation—such as color accents, varied textures, sounds, pleasant smells, and interactive features. It also assesses whether the environment may introduce negative sensory experiences or is supportive of varying sensory needs such as opportunities for sensory retreat or reduced stimulation.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve the sensory qualitites of this playspace:

questions.prompt: has integrated **color in ways that creates visual interest** (e.g. colored play features or accents; art; flowering plants)

scales.title: Diversity

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

scales.title: Provision

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

questions.prompt: provides opportunities to **experience or create sounds** (e.g. musical instruments; motion/weight-activated sound devices; wind chimes; talking tubes; flowing water; wildlife sounds; rustling grasses)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

scales.title: Sociability Support
scales.prompt: Can more than one child or person use this feature/environmental characteristic together?

questions.prompt: provides **tactile experiences through different textures, materials and/or vibration** (e.g. sand or mud play; soft-textured vegetation; musical elements that vibrate)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

scales.title: Diversity
scales.prompt: To what extent is there diversity in the provision of this feature/environmental characteristic?

questions.prompt: has **places for retreat or withdrawa** l from sensory stimulation (e.g., small or enclosed spaces; nature-rich areas; isolated quiet / away spaces; quiet/passive zones separated from loud/active zones)

scales.title: Provision

questions.prompt: has **consistent noise pollution** (e.g. from heavy traffic, trains, or loud music; pervasive winds; unpredictable noises)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Accommodating diverse abilities
sections.description: **Accommodating Diverse Abilities:** examines how well a playspace supports children with diverse physical, sensory, and communication needs. It focuses on the accessibility and usability of play features, paths, and transitions; the availability of tools that help children express their needs; and the presence of design elements that enable participation from a wide range of body positions and abilities.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve this playspace for diverse ages and abilities.

questions.prompt: has designated **accessible parking** (for motor vehicles and/or bicycles)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: ...has **digital or analog communication boards** available that help children to communicate their needs and wishes (the communication board should reflect the play opportunities and amenities available in the play space)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: ... **has manufactured play features with accessible access** (e.g. features designed to accommodate users with mobility aids, TODO THOAMS: put example here )

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **has accessible edges or points of access** (flush or ramped) from the primary path to accessible play surfaces or directly onto play features.

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: **highest points and/or the "coolest" / most unique play features are accessible via ramps or use of topography**

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has **accessible manufactured play features that are isolated** from other play areas, behind fencing or visual barriers (e.g., fenced-in wheelchair swings)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: play features **support transitions in and out** (e.g., entry/exit ramps; ample space and/or platforms to transfer from a mobility device; slides with extended run-outs at the base for easy transfer)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: ... play features have **handrails and grips to assist with transitions and stability** that are easy to use for children with different abilities (e.g., located at various heights; in sizes and shapes suitable for different-sized hands)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: play features allow for **use from diverse body positions**, providing appropriate support for children with different needs (e.g., options for sitting, standing, or lying down; seats or swings with full-body support, wide bases, and/or secure straps; footrests so feet do not have to dangle)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

questions.prompt: has clear, **tactile, and/or visual indicators** (e.g. color contrasts, material changes, or textured/dimpled surfaces, low raised boundaries) to define edges of paths or signal significant changes in height (e.g. edges of elevated platform) or transitions into new spaces (e.g. into the swing areas)

scales.title: Provision
scales.prompt: To what degree is this feature/environmental characteristic present or considered?

sections.title: Playspace suitability for diverse users
sections.description: **Playspace suitability for diverse users:** assesses how well the playspace as a whole meets the needs of children across a wide range of ages and abilities.
sections.instruction: Read each statement and answer the questions. This playspace...
sections.notes_prompt: Any comments? Describe one or more recommendations to improve this playspace for diverse ages and abilities.
